Toggle navigation Exserohilum turcicum (SETOTU) Menu Overview → Photos Documents Overview Code created in: 2002-02-03 Basic information EPPO Code: SETOTU Preferred name: Exserohilum turcicum Authority: (Passerini) K.J.Leonard & E.G.Suggs Other scientific names Name Authority Bipolaris turcica (Passerini) Shoemaker Bipolaris turcicum Shoemaker Drechslera turcica Subramanian & Jain Helminthosporium inconspicuum Cooke & Ellis Helminthosporium turcicum Passerini Setosphaeria turcica (Luttrell) K.J.Leonard & E.G.Suggs Trichometasphaeria turcica Luttrell Common names Name Language leaf blight of maize English leaf blight of sorghum English northern corn leaf blight English northern leaf blight of maize English Blattdürre: Mais German Streifenkrankheit: Mais German brûlure du maïs French brûlure helminthosporienne du maïs French helminthosporiose du maïs French suie du maïs French tizón de las hojas: maíz Spanish kukurūzų dryžligė Lithuanian more photos... Taxonomy Kingdom Fungi      (
      1FUNGK      ) Phylum Ascomycota      (
      1ASCOP      ) Subphylum Pezizomycotina      (
      1PEZIQ      ) Class Dothideomycetes      (
      1DOTHC      ) Subclass Pleosporomycetidae      (
      1PLEOL      ) Order Pleosporales      (
      1PLEOO      ) Family Pleosporaceae      (
      1PLEOF      ) Genus Exserohilum      (
      1EXSEG      ) Species Exserohilum turcicum      (
      SETOTU      )

seed.symptoms: Long (up to 15 cm), cigar-shaped gray-green to tan lesions on leaves; may kill entire leaves and reduce yield significantly.
seed.causes: Fungus Exserohilum turcicum; overwinters in crop residue; spreads via wind-borne conidia in cool, moist conditions.
seed.treatment: Fungicide applications (triazoles, strobilurins) at tasseling if disease is severe.
seed.prevention: Crop rotation; resistant hybrids; residue management.
